feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Google is releasing millions of mosquitoes in these states. See why
entry.summary: You don't need to stock up on repellent, or worry about getting more mosquito bites, these male insects don't feed on humans.

entry.title: Trump Administration Tries to Shift Blame for Ebola Response
entry.summary: After cutting its support for front-line healthcare workers in Central Africa, the Trump administration is pointing fingers.

entry.title: Roundup: Ebola outbreak / Mosquito problem / AI doctors
entry.summary: World Cup challenge: As athletes and fans prepare for the FIFA World Cup starting next week, global health officials are gearing up to protect against infectious diseases. For the first time, the tournament will span 16 host cities across three countries, the…

entry.title: This Habit Could Lead to Early-Onset Dementia, New Study Suggests
entry.summary: Heavy drinking is linked to early-onset dementia, a new study shows. Learn how alcohol affects brain health and what you can do to lower your risk.

entry.title: Marriage sex-life advice: I just discovered the truth about the beginnings of my relationship. I’m devastated.
entry.summary: Prudie says: “that’s why I’m writing an advice column rather than running a private practice.”

entry.title: Google Aims to Debug California and Florida by Releasing 64 Million Mosquitoes
entry.summary: An earlier project already led to a 95 percent drop in biting females of one disease-carrying species in Fresno.

entry.title: Tainted Food Is Killing Far More People Than Previously Thought, WHO Says
entry.summary: The WHO's report provides an updated estimate on the global toll of foodborne disease.

entry.title: Ultra-processed food harms tied to content not manufacture, review says
entry.summary: Some UPFs are benign, while others are unhealthy because of their contents, according to a study published in Science

entry.title: Erie County resident tests positive for measles
entry.summary: The Erie County Department of Health reports a confirmed measles case, the first since 2018.

entry.title: Rare type of Lyme disease found for the first time in New York
entry.summary: A rare type of Lyme disease caused by B. mayonii bacteria was found for the first time in New York state. It was previously only ever detected in Minnesota and Wisconsin.

entry.title: Health department tracking possible tuberculosis exposures tied to Tri-State school
entry.summary: The NKY Health Department is trying to track down people who may have been exposed to tuberculosis

entry.title: Ticks are thriving in Minnesota. Experts explain why.
entry.summary: Health experts emphasize early detection and prevention as tick activity increases statewide.

entry.title: Measles Outbreak in a Child Care Facility — Lubbock, Texas, March–April 2025
entry.summary: This report describes eight confirmed measles cases linked to a child care facility in Lubbock, Texas.

entry.title: Opinion | Ebola is spreading in ways no one fully grasps. Use what I know.
entry.summary: As cases rapidly grow, a massive deployment of resources is critical.

entry.title: "Breaking New Ground": Man Reveals Results After Injecting Himself With Unregulated Tanning Peptide Melanotan 2
entry.summary: A viral tweet on X claimed that the man was so desperate to achieve a tanned appearance that he jabbed himself with an unregulated medication.

entry.title: What will it take to get a vaccine for the Ebola strain driving the current outbreak?
entry.summary: There is an effective vaccine for Ebola — but not for the variety spreading rapidly in the Democratic Republic of Congo. Trials are going on for several candidates. How long will it take?

entry.title: Your Grip Strength Says a Lot About How Long You Might Live
entry.summary: If you follow wellness channels on social media, you might've come across the claim that your grip strength – or how firmly you can squeeze something with your hands – can predict how long you will live.

entry.title: Michigan found a way to reduce school vaccine waivers. until it backfired.
entry.summary: State health officials urged parents in several counties to vaccinate babies against measles ahead of schedule this spring as cases multiplied in Michigan. The outbreaks of the highly contagious virus…

entry.title: We won’t know what screen time is doing to kids’ brains for decades, expert warns
entry.summary: Need a reason not to give your child a screen? It may permanently alter their brain during a critical period of development researchers have named the ‘criticome.’

entry.title: Golden Retriever Finds a ‘Baby’ on Sidewalk, Carries It Home & Refuses To Let Go
entry.summary: Walking past a pile of neighborhood trash can suddenly turn into a treasure hunt if you have the right attitude. That’s exactly what happened when Bowser, a...

entry.title: Cardiologist: 5 things I never do before 9 a.m.—after 20 years of treating heart attacks
entry.summary: Dr. Sanjay Bhojraj, a cardiologist who has treated heart disease for over 20 years, shares the morning habits he avoids to protect cardiovascular health and reduce unnecessary stress on the body. Instead, his routine centers on protein, fiber, hydration and s…

entry.title: Woman Has Baby Using Sperm Donor—Horror at What She Discovers Months Later
entry.summary: Sperm donor reveals woman's son has rare genetic disorder.

entry.title: A Single Lifestyle Change Could Lower Your Risk of Dementia by 16%
entry.summary: What's bad for the lungs may also be bad for the brain.

entry.title: What Happens to Your Visceral Fat When You Walk Every Day
entry.summary: Walking is an easy, effective way to reduce visceral fat. Fitness experts share why walking helps and how much you need to walk to see meaningful results.

entry.title: 90-120 minutes of strength training per week may help extend lifespan
entry.summary: A new study has revealed the ideal exercise combo and time to protect the heart and mind, finding that the sweet spot may be around 90-120 minutes of two types of exercises.

entry.title: A New Biological Clock Could Predict When You'll Probably Die
entry.summary: Make the most of it.

entry.title: As the largest World Cup ever kicks off, health officials are focused on more than Ebola
entry.summary: Officials are more concerned about highly contagious diseases like measles and respiratory viruses, which can spread quickly through large, fast-moving crowds.

entry.title: 5 ways to find purpose and stay active during retirement
entry.summary: Infusing retirement with meaning can help people make the most of this new phase of life, experts say.

entry.title: This Quirky Accessory Can Actually Make Walking Easier And Boost Heart Health
entry.summary: Reviewers love this pair of insanely highly-rated walking sticks.

entry.title: RFK Jr. seeks to peek at Americans’ medical records for clues on autism and vaccines
entry.summary: U.S. health secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is pursuing federal government access to most Americans’ medical records, in a quest to research a link between vaccines and autism — a connection the medical establishment studied for decades and flatly rejects.

entry.title: Male puberty is understudied — but when it starts may predict long-term health risks
entry.summary: Due to a significant gender gap, far less is known about the health risks of early or late puberty onset among boys than girls.

entry.title: Cancer’s favorite escape trick may actually make it easier to kill
entry.summary: Scientists have uncovered a surprising new way the immune system fights cancer, overturning a core belief that has guided immunology for decades. The research found that when cancer cells shut down a key immune-recognition molecule called MHC I—a common trick…

entry.title: This mysterious lung disease affects millions of people — but a drug tested in mice shows promise
entry.summary: The therapy activates a protein that could stop pulmonary fibrosis from progressing.

entry.title: People Are Revealing Their Easy And Practical Protein Tips, And I'm Adding Them To My Notes App
entry.summary: "Beans for the win!"

entry.title: The US Just Updated Its Guidelines to Detect And Prevent Colorectal Cancer
entry.summary: It's increasingly common to see headlines and social media conversations about the rise in colorectal cancer among younger adults.

entry.title: A Customer Support Agent Noticed a Terrifying Trend of Customer Delusions—Until He Deployed a Genius Back-Up Method
entry.summary: The best of the internet, sifted, and sorted.

entry.title: Ebola Burial Team Attacked, 11 Patients Flee Care in Widening Outbreak in Congo
entry.summary: A burial team was attacked and 11 Ebola patients fled isolation facilities in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, as the outbreak spread to another health zone in the country’s hardest-hit province.

entry.title: Breakthrough ovarian cancer drug offers patients more time and better quality of life
entry.summary: Women taking the drug tell the BBC it has given them their lives back.

entry.title: Why Google wants to release 32 million mosquitoes in California
entry.summary: Google wants the EPA's OK to try a pest control method to quell the spread of an invasive mosquito species that spreads dengue and other serious diseases.

entry.title: Cat Ownership Linked to Increased Risk of Schizophrenia, Study Suggests
entry.summary: Having a cat as a pet is linked to an increased risk of schizophrenia-related conditions, according to a 2023 analysis of 17 studies.

entry.title: Unsafe food causes 866 million illnesses and 1.5 million deaths annually, young children at highest risk
entry.summary:
Children aged less than five years face almost three times the risk of illness from unsafe food than older children and adults, according to new estimates released today by the World Health Organization (WHO).

Despite being just 9% of the global pop…

entry.title: A new pancreatic cancer drug is changing survival rates for one of the deadliest cancers
entry.summary: A new pancreatic cancer drug, daraxonrasib, is showing impressive results, nearly doubling survival in early trials. Here’s what doctors say.

entry.title: A natural depsipeptide antibiotic binds the E-site of the bacterial ribosome
entry.summary: Improved fractionation strategies can identify antibiotics with previously unseen scaffolds and mechanisms, exemplified by manikomycin from Streptomyces rimosus, which acts by targeting the E-site of the bacterial large ribosomal subunit.

entry.title: Cell-type-resolved genetic variation shapes inflammatory bowel disease risk
entry.summary: Single-cell mapping of cis-expression quantitative trait loci in inflammatory bowel disease revealed distal, enhancer-enriched variants detected at the cell-type level more frequently co-localize with genome-wide association study loci than those id…

entry.title: Acquired genetic and cell-state changes in IDH-mutant glioma progression
entry.summary: Longitudinal transcriptomic, chromatin and genomic analyses of the two types of IDH-mutant glioma reveal in detail how disease progression is influenced by interdependent genetic, epigenetic and microenvironmental factors.

entry.title: Why the Brain Doesn’t Need Choices to Generate Intent
entry.summary: A new study rejects the sandwich model, proving the brain lacks a decision-making center.

entry.title: Tick-borne illnesses are on the rise in Oregon, and one can make you allergic to red meat
entry.summary: As hospitalization rates rise across the country, Oregon is facing one of its worst surges of the last decade.

entry.title: Google wants to release 64 million bacteria-riddled mosquitoes across California and Florida. Here’s why scientists are enthusiastic.
entry.summary: Google has applied for an experimental mosquito release permit to deploy millions of non-biting southern house mosquitoes that it has infected with the bacterium Wolbachia pipientis, in an effort to reduce mosquito-borne diseases like West Nile virus.

entry.title: Human Rabies Exposure Reported As 1st Bat Tests Positive In McHenry County
entry.summary: Health officials say a resident received preventive treatment after finding a bat inside a home and are urging residents to take precautions

entry.title: The 2 hours-a-week weights plan
entry.summary: A new study shows resistance training can cut the risk of Alzheimer’s. Here’s how to get started
